title: What My Sister Buried
theme: A town never forgets. It just redecorates.

cast.name: Derek Bell
traits: owns a structured coat for crisis scenes
replies to texts while processing betrayal
secret_capacity: once left town without explaining why
cast.regional_responsibility: serves on a board no one fully understands
cast.space_description: unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files
cast.suspicion_profile: can host Thanksgiving but could move a body-shaped rug

cast.name: Luke Dane
traits: forgot a recital once and never recovered
secret_capacity: covered for someone out of shame
knows where evidence should have been
cast.regional_responsibility: organizes a fundraiser with tense undertones
cast.space_description: dock office with a desk nobody fully claims
cast.suspicion_profile: can host Thanksgiving but could move a body-shaped rug

cast.name: Daniel Cross
traits: appears exactly when gossip is needed
says the dangerous thing out loud
secret_capacity: knows about a half sibling no one asked for
cast.regional_responsibility: serves on a board no one fully understands
cast.space_description: unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files
cast.suspicion_profile: knows where a secret could fit architecturally

cast.name: Sienna Lark
secret_capacity: inherited documents she should have burned
knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events
cast.space_description: retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence
cast.suspicion_profile: can host Thanksgiving but could move a body-shaped rug

cast.name: Derek Pike
traits: briefly leaves the room after difficult questions
secret_capacity: has a storage unit he refuses to explain
moved money during a family emergency
cast.regional_responsibility: serves on a board no one fully understands
cast.space_description: annex room described as paperwork I have not gone through yet

cast.name: Rachel Bennett
secret_capacity: accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
cast.regional_responsibility: serves on a board no one fully understands
cast.space_description: storage unit described as old family things

cast.name: Tess Pike
traits: notices timeline errors before adults do

cast.name: Wyatt Pruitt
traits: knows the old case too well

beat_sheet.text: Derek Bell relocates to Winter Hollow after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Rachel Bennett hesitates before explaining storage unit described as old family things. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Tess Pike hates the move and immediately distrusts Luke Dane, a high school coach with suspiciously healthy handyman energy and access to dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Derek Bell hesitates before explaining unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

A wine scene at The Candle Finch introduces the casserole dish, local gossip about the woman from the orchard road, and the fact that everybody here has somewhere to hide from a conversation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Derek Bell hesitates before explaining unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Sienna Lark reveals a perfectly phrased warning in a well-lit kitchen while phone static saying her name, then declines to explain retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

Someone goes missing after mentioning the unopened letter, the old orchard chapel, and retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence in the same sentence. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.84.

The reveal ties snow globe to Blackthorn Vineyard, St. Agnes Regional, and a brunch resurrection event by routing through retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence instead of one simple basement. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.97.

Tess Pike spots the timeline error that every adult has spiritually rented around and asks why everybody in this county owns just storage. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Sienna Lark hesitates before explaining retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.25.

Sienna Lark confesses just enough to make the truth worse while the sheriff arrives exactly fourteen minutes too late and someone stares through the dusty window of dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

Sequel bait lands when the ceramic owl reappears and someone whispers about the Winterson case, the vineyard fire, and another offsite room nobody has adequately explained. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Derek Pike hesitates before explaining annex room described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. contradiction_ledger.entry: Luke Dane is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Derek Bell says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Alder Ridge.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Luke?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Tess. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Derek. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The casserole dish is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Sienna."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
fake_critic_blurb: "What My Sister Buried understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "What My Sister Buried" follows Derek Bell, an overextended professional woman who mistakes productivity for safety until emotionally neglected marriage collides with Luke Dane, a comforting regional adult carrying portable suspicion and plausible access to offsite storage. As phone static saying her name, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Where every secret comes home.
